(Spanish pronunciation: [[toreˈon]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:ES-pe_-_Torre%C3%B3n.ogg "File:ES-pe - Torreón.ogg")) is a city and seat of [Torreón Municipality](/wiki/Torre%C3%B3n_Municipality "Torreón Municipality") in the [Mexican](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") [state](/wiki/States_of_Mexico "States of Mexico") of [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"). The city's population is 720,848 inhabitants, making it the second largest city in the state of [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"). Torreón is part of the [Comarca Lagunera](/wiki/Comarca_Lagunera "Comarca Lagunera") metropolitan area. It borders the cites of [Gómez Palacio](/wiki/G%C3%B3mez_Palacio,_Durango "Gómez Palacio, Durango") and [Lerdo](/wiki/Lerdo,_Durango "Lerdo, Durango") to the west.

**Torreón** (Spanish pronunciation: [[toreˈon]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:ES-pe_-_Torre%C3%B3n.ogg "File:ES-pe - Torreón.ogg")) is a city and seat of [Torreón Municipality](/wiki/Torre%C3%B3n_Municipality "Torreón Municipality") in the [Mexican](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") [state](/wiki/States_of_Mexico "States of Mexico") of [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"). The city's population is 720,848 inhabitants, making it the second largest city in the state of [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"). Torreón is part of the [Comarca Lagunera](/wiki/Comarca_Lagunera "Comarca Lagunera") metropolitan area. It borders the cites of [Gómez Palacio](/wiki/G%C3%B3mez_Palacio,_Durango "Gómez Palacio, Durango") and [Lerdo](/wiki/Lerdo,_Durango "Lerdo, Durango") to the west.

The area was originally a center for ranching. With irrigation, the city became an important center for farming and the processing of cotton. The city's economy is based on the metals, livestock, agriculture industries, the textile, metallurgical, chemical, commerce and industries. It is one of the youngest cities in Mexico, having celebrated its centenary in 2007. It is popularly nicknamed "The City of Great Efforts" because despite all the adverse environmental conditions, it managed to flourish as a city due to its prosperous industry and commerce.

Torreón is served by [Francisco Sarabia International Airport](/wiki/Francisco_Sarabia_International_Airport "Francisco Sarabia International Airport"), an airport with flights to several cities in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") and the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

According to archaeological findings, the area of Torreón was populated around the 10th millennium BC.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a7/%C3%9Anico_y_%C3%BAltimo_torre%C3%B3n_en_Torre%C3%B3n%2C_capital_del_estado_de_Coahuila..jpg/250px-%C3%9Anico_y_%C3%BAltimo_torre%C3%B3n_en_Torre%C3%B3n%2C_capital_del_estado_de_Coahuila..jpg)](/wiki/File:%C3%9Anico_y_%C3%BAltimo_torre%C3%B3n_en_Torre%C3%B3n,_capital_del_estado_de_Coahuila..jpg)

One of the few remaining towers (Torreones) in the city.

The first Spanish mission arrived in 1566, led by Fr. Pedro Espinareda. However, the city developed only in the independent Mexican era, around a *Torreón* ("Big Tower") built to monitor Río Nazas's floods, in conjunction with the creation of a railroad connecting to the US border city of [El Paso](/wiki/El_Paso "El Paso"), which gave an economic boom to the city and therefore a population boom as well. The population grew from 200 in 1892 to 34,000 in 1910. Torreón received city status in 1907.

During the [Mexican Revolution](/wiki/Mexican_Revolution "Mexican Revolution") (1910–1920), the city was taken more than once; the most prominent character ever to take the city was the revolutionary general [Pancho Villa](/wiki/Pancho_Villa "Pancho Villa"). It was also the location of the 1911 [Torreón massacre](/wiki/Torre%C3%B3n_massacre "Torreón massacre"), where 303 Chinese immigrants were killed by the revolutionaries over a ten-hour period.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) During the revolution, Torreón was also the site of an important convention that led to a deal between the rebellious armies. The city is 56 km (35 mi) southwest of historic [San Pedro de las Colonias](/wiki/San_Pedro_de_las_Colonias "San Pedro de las Colonias"), where some of the Mexican Revolution battles occurred.

After the Mexican Revolution, the city continued to develop economically; during the first years of the 20th century, the primary industry of the city was farming, although other industries later formed or were established in the area.

On September 15, 2007, Torreón celebrated its first 100 years as a chartered city. It held a series of cultural events from September 15, 2006, to September 15, 2007, culminating on the day that the city turned 100 years old.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Torreón is near the southwest border of the state of Coahuila, within the [Laguna](/wiki/Comarca_Lagunera "Comarca Lagunera") region of the state. The border is delineated by the [Nazas River](/wiki/R%C3%ADo_Nazas "Río Nazas") that separates it from Gómez Palacio, Durango. The municipality covers 1,947.7 km2 (752.0 sq mi), including much of the rural area south of the city. The city's elevation is 1,120 m (3,670 ft), which is low for the Mexican interior. The terrain where the urban area is spread is generally flat, with somewhat prominent relief formations (up to 1,600 m (5,249 ft) south and southwest of the city, thus visible at well-nigh any given point in the city. Higher mountains, over 3,000 m (9,843 ft), are on the southern, mostly uninhabited section of the Municipality, the most prominent being El Picacho.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

The city features a [desert](/wiki/Desert "Desert") climate (in the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *BWh*). Rainfall is scarce but more prominent in the summer, whilst temperatures are very hot by day and cool at night, although the [urban heat island](/wiki/Urban_heat_island "Urban heat island") effect causes temperatures on summer nights to be considerably warmer than nearby areas. Flora and fauna are those common to [semidesert](/wiki/Semidesert "Semidesert") habitats.

## Main sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Main sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a7/Canal_de_la_Perla_Torreon_Coah.jpg/250px-Canal_de_la_Perla_Torreon_Coah.jpg)](/wiki/File:Canal_de_la_Perla_Torreon_Coah.jpg)

El Canal de la Perla.

*[Cristo de las Noas](/wiki/Cristo_de_las_Noas "Cristo de las Noas")*, at 21.80 m (71.5 ft), is the third tallest statue of Christ in [Latin America](/wiki/Latin_America "Latin America"), only smaller than the statue of *[Christ The Redeemer](/wiki/Christ_the_Redeemer_(statue) "Christ the Redeemer (statue)")* in [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") and *[Cristo de la Concordia](/wiki/Cristo_de_la_Concordia "Cristo de la Concordia")* in [Cochabamba](/wiki/Cochabamba "Cochabamba"), [Bolivia](/wiki/Bolivia "Bolivia"). Situated on the top of a hill, this image of Jesus with extended arms symbolises protection for the inhabitants of Torreon.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The hilltop has a Catholic church and offers a view of the entire city.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

There are also several shopping centers in the city, including Galerias Laguna, Plaza Cuatro Caminos, and Intermall.

The "Canal de la Perla" (the Pearl Watercourse), an underground watercourse built in the 19th century to drive the Nazas' river water to the fields near the city, was re-discovered in 2003 and re-opened in 2014. It now passes under the oldest part of the city and it can be visited and walked through. It is also used for cultural and artistic exhibitions.

In 2006, the "Museo Arocena" (Arocena Museum) that holds art collections from the pre-Hispanic times to the present, was opened; it also has a section dedicated to Mexico's and Torreón's history. There are also temporal expositions, conferences, book fairs, movies, and activities for children.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

The area was originally a center for ranching. With irrigation the city became an important center for support for farming and processing of cotton. In the middle of the 20th century, it became an industrial city. The city has industries in textiles, clothing and metals processing. Some important industries and companies that have business here, like [Peñoles](/wiki/Pe%C3%B1oles "Peñoles"), an important Mexican mining group, and [Motores John Deere](/wiki/Deere_%26_Company "Deere & Company"), Lala, an important dairy products company, Yura Corporation, stores like Soriana, Cimaco, Extra, among others.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sports")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Un_partido_de_Santos_Laguna_vs._Gimnasia_de_La_Plata.jpg/250px-Un_partido_de_Santos_Laguna_vs._Gimnasia_de_La_Plata.jpg)](/wiki/File:Un_partido_de_Santos_Laguna_vs._Gimnasia_de_La_Plata.jpg)

[Estadio Corona](/wiki/Estadio_Corona "Estadio Corona").

Torreón has a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team in the [Liga MX](/wiki/Liga_MX "Liga MX") named [Santos Laguna](/wiki/Santos_Laguna "Santos Laguna"). The team won championships in 1996, 2001, 2008, 2012, 2015, and 2018. It used to play in [Estadio Corona](/wiki/Estadio_Corona "Estadio Corona"), until 2009 when it moved to the [Territorio Santos Modelo](/wiki/Territorio_Santos_Modelo "Territorio Santos Modelo") to meet the growing demands of its fan base.

The city is also home to a [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") team called "[Vaqueros Laguna](/wiki/Vaqueros_Laguna "Vaqueros Laguna")" (Laguna Cowboys) of the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League"). They play at [Estadio Revolución](/wiki/Estadio_Revoluci%C3%B3n "Estadio Revolución").

Torreón's professional basketball team, [Jefes de Fuerza Lagunera](/wiki/Jefes_de_Fuerza_Lagunera "Jefes de Fuerza Lagunera"), play in the Municipal Auditorium, which seats approximately 3,000 people. They are members of the LNBP ([Liga Nacional de Baloncesto Profesional](/wiki/Liga_Nacional_de_Baloncesto_Profesional "Liga Nacional de Baloncesto Profesional")) which is considered to be the top basketball league in Mexico.

Former [NFL](/wiki/NFL "NFL") placekicker and [Super Bowl XXI](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XXI "Super Bowl XXI") champion, [Raul Allegre](/wiki/Raul_Allegre "Raul Allegre"), is a Torreón native.

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Events")]

### Cotton and Grape Fair

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Cotton and Grape Fair")]

The main annual festival in Torreón is the Cotton and Grape Fair (Feria del Algodón y La Uva) which takes place in September. It contains cultural events, music, food and amusement rides.

### Independence Day

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Torre%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Independence Day")]

It takes place on September 15 and 16. There are celebrations all around the city, but the most important is the one celebrated in the Plaza Mayor. People wear traditional Mexican clothes, eat traditional dishes and "Antojitos". At night the city mayor makes the traditional celebration of "El Grito". The next day (September 16) there is a parade on the Morelos Avenue.
